name: Classic Cheesecake with Cinnamon Crumb Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine graham cracker crumbs, granulated sugar, and ground cinnamon.
2. Stir in melted butter until the mixture is well combined. Additional butter may be added up to 3/4 cup if needed.
3. Grease an 8-inch springform pan on the bottom and sides with more melted butter.
4. Sprinkle a thin layer of the crumb mixture over the bottom of the pan and press down using a metal measuring cup (1/4-cup measure).
5. Pat the crumbs onto the sides of the pan and press into place with the side of the measuring cup. Work around the sides to ensure an even height.
6. Any remaining crumb mixture can be added to the bottom crust.
7. Bake the crust at 350°F (175°C) for 5 minutes.
8. In a separate bowl, beat eggs until light. Gradually add granulated sugar and continue beating for 5 minutes.
9. Add softened cream cheese, vanilla extract, and Grand Marnier; mix well.
10. Pour the filling into the prepared crust and bake at 375°F (190°C) for 20 minutes.
11. Allow the cheesecake to cool for 15 minutes before removing from the pan.
12. To make the topping, blend sour cream, granulated sugar, vanilla extract, and Grand Marnier in a bowl until smooth.
13. Pour the sour cream mixture over the warm filling and return the cheesecake to the oven.
14. Bake at 475°F (245°C) for an additional 5 minutes.
15. Remove from the oven and let the cheesecake cool completely before refrigerating overnight.
